summary: The suite covers all five core functions and the full integration pipeline, matching every stated requirement: mock data structure, threshold filtering (CPU-only, memory-only, OR logic), top-N sorting, report content, and the no-alerts case. Organization is clean — TDD phases are clearly labeled, BeforeEach fixtures are properly scoped, and dependency injection via -ProcessData is correctly validated. The main weaknesses are in rigor: (1) no boundary-condition test for processes exactly *at* the threshold (the implementation uses -gt, so at-threshold processes are excluded — a subtle but testable behaviour); (2) Get-FilteredProcesses is never exercised with a null or single-element input, where PowerShell's scalar-vs-array pipeline behaviour can silently break things; (3) Get-TopResourceConsumers with an empty array or TopN=0 is not tested; (4) the null-normalisation path in New-AlertReport is implemented but untested; (5) the type assertion for CPU/MemoryMB only inspects the first element of the mock array, not all entries. Despite these gaps, the suite would reliably catch most functional regressions in the happy-path and common error paths.
